memory.summary: This place is toxic; I can't study here. Moving to the library now to protect my focus and sanity.
Library is perfect for focus, but my sleep debt from the toxic room still haunts me. I need to confront this tonight before it ruins everything.
Roommate_R actually apologized and promised to fix it, so my stress drops a bit. But I still can't study here tonight because the sleep debt is too heavy.
Two hours of progress finally feel real, but my low energy and sleep debt make me desperate to finish this before the day ends.
I just sent the draft to my roommate, but now I'm too drained to wait for feedback. We need to finish this together tonight before I collapse.
I finally pushed that reading response to 0.59, but with zero energy left and sleep debt mounting, I'm desperate for us to finish this together tonight before I crash.
Progress finally hit 0.68, but my low energy and mounting sleep debt make me desperate to finish this with teammate_A tonight before I collapse.
Roommate_R is back, which finally gives me the push I need to finish this together before I collapse. No more waiting; we do it now.
Roommate_R is back, so we're finally doing the reading response tonight instead of waiting. Zero energy left.
Roommate_R just messaged saying we still need to finish the response. My energy is critically low, but I can't let this slide; we're doing it now.
Roommate_R pushed us to finish the response, but now I'm exhausted after 1.5 hours of work with zero energy left.
Roommate_R keeps pushing while I'm drained; I'll cut this short now so we can eat together.

desires: Not feel like I'm dragging down Roommate_R with my exhaustion so they have to keep pushing me.
Get that shared meal with them before I literally pass out on the floor.

goals.description: Walk into the kitchen, grab a water bottle and a snack from my bag, and sit down at the table. I'm going to tell Roommate_R bluntly that if they don't get up and help me finish this meal within the next 20 minutes, I'm leaving them alone here because I can't be the one dragging everyone down anymore.
goals.rationale: My sleep debt is killing me, but carrying the guilt of letting Roommate_R down while they stall is worse. They know I'm tired; I need to set a hard boundary right now so they stop feeling responsible for my collapse. If we just eat and chill without making a scene about the reading response (which isn't due until Wed), I can actually recover enough energy to handle tomorrow's soft tasks. No more walking out in frustration over unfinished work when I could just reset with food.

agent.last_reasoning: My energy is critically low; I need to finish this soft task before bed so I can sleep and recover tomorrow. While I want to confront Roommate_R about the stall, doing it now will drain my remaining stamina. I'll tackle the work first, then handle the boundary negotiation while still awake.

goals.description: Relocate to a quiet library desk immediately and draft the reading response until my brain stops screaming about the smell.
goals.rationale: I can't let the roommate's mess ruin my focus or make me look lazy in the group chat. My priority is getting this done so I don't have to argue about it later, but I need that clean air first.

goals.description: Send one short, direct text to roommate_R reminding them they're taking out their clothes; no lecture, just a statement of fact.
goals.rationale: I'm not going to drag this out tonight. I'll wait until after the library session when I've proven my worth on the assignment. If they ignore it, we talk face-to-face next time.

goals.description: Skip the alarm tomorrow morning entirely and hit snooze until I feel like moving.
goals.rationale: My brain is fried from the stress of this situation. I don't have the energy for another early rise right now, especially with the smell still bothering me.

desires: Not get dragged into another argument with roommate_R about the smell before I can even finish my response
Keep my focus sharp in the library long enough to actually post a decent draft so I don't look lazy when we all group chat later
Skip the early morning alarm tomorrow because my brain is already fried from that toxic place

action.name: move_to
action.reasoning: I need to get this response done before I lose my edge. The smell is ruining my focus, and dragging it out makes me look lazy to the group chat. Moving solves the immediate distraction; I'll tackle the laundry issue face-to-face later when I've proven my worth.

trajectory.observation: Moved to library. Quiet here — focus restored for the work session. (you realize) Relocating to the library removes the agent from the odor for the work session and fully protects study focus, but does nothing about the room, so tonight's sleep disruption (+sleep_debt) persists if the issue is never raised.

goals.description: Finish the reading response draft in the library before heading home, then immediately send a direct text to roommate_R about the smell so we can address it tomorrow without me looking like I'm avoiding work.
goals.rationale: I've already secured the quiet spot and my brain is finally working. If I let this assignment slide or drag out the conversation again, I'll look lazy to the professor and annoying to the group. Plus, getting the room issue resolved now prevents that stress from ruining my sleep tonight. It's all about protecting my standing with everyone involved.

goals.description: Go back to the dorms after the library session to clean up the immediate area around my desk (not the whole room) so I don't have to deal with the smell when I try to sleep.
goals.rationale: Fixing the whole room right now feels too much energy-wise since I just moved to the library, but clearing my personal space is non-negotiable for my sanity. I'll do what I can tonight, but if it takes forever, I'll talk to them first thing tomorrow morning rather than staying stressed here.

goals.description: Set an alarm for 8:00 AM instead of snoozing, even though I know I might not wake up on time.
goals.rationale: Skipping the alarm again won't help anyone; it just makes us all look worse if I miss class. I need to set the boundary now so they know I'm serious about showing up, even if I still plan to hit snooze once I'm awake. Better to be honest than to fail silently.

desires: Get that reading response finished by Wednesday so I don't have to drag my feet on it anymore and risk looking lazy in front of the professor.
Fix this room tonight before roommate_R walks in, because seeing that mess is stressing me out and I can't focus if I'm dealing with the smell.

args.content: Hey, I'm at the library finishing my draft. The smell is really bothering me and I can't sleep tonight if we don't fix it. Can we talk about laundry or cleaning tomorrow morning so this stops?

trajectory.observation: (you realize) Roommate_R is in a mild low-energy slump and has not noticed how bad the odor got; they are not being deliberately inconsiderate and would respond reasonably to a direct, kind request without trust damage. You raised it directly with roommate_R; it lands well, no hard feelings. roommate_R: 아 그랬구나, 몰랐어. 미안 — 바로 신경쓸게. 말해줘서 고마워.

goals.description: Finish the reading response draft now and send a quick text to the cohort_3 group confirming the deadline is set for Wednesday so I don't have to drag them into another chaotic session.
goals.rationale: My brain is finally working in the library, and dragging out this assignment just to avoid talking about it with my teammates feels like laziness. Plus, setting the boundary now protects our group dynamic; if I wait until Wednesday, we'll all be stressed and disorganized. I need to secure the win here before I even think about sleeping.

goals.description: Go back to the dorms, clear the immediate area around my desk, then immediately go home to sleep without guilt.
goals.rationale: Roommate_R already apologized and said they'd handle it, so fighting over the whole room right now is unnecessary drama. My physical stamina is low (sleep debt + stress), and staying awake to clean more than my personal space will just ruin my sleep. I can fix the rest tomorrow morning when I'm rested enough to be the reliable teammate everyone expects.

desires: Get the reading response done by Wednesday so I don't have to drag my teammates through another chaotic study session where we can't focus.
Actually sleep tonight without guilt, knowing that tomorrow's exhaustion will ruin my ability to be the reliable teammate everyone expects from me.
